name: HDFC Freedom Credit Card

fees.waiverCondition: Spend ₹50,000 or more in the previous year

categoryRates.description: 1 CashBack Point per ₹150 on all retail purchases
5X CashBack Points on dining spends (5 points per ₹150)
5X CashBack Points on grocery spends (5 points per ₹150)
2X CashBack Points on telecom & utility bill payments

additionalBenefits: 5X rewards on dining and grocery categories
2X rewards on telecom and utility bills
pros: 5X rewards on dining and grocery are useful for everyday spending
Very low joining and annual fee of ₹500
Easy fee waiver threshold of ₹50,000 annual spend
Good entry card with low income requirement of ₹2L
2X on telecom helps with recurring utility payments
cons: Base reward rate of 1 point per ₹150 (0.33%) is low
3.5% forex markup makes it unsuitable for international use
idealFor: Budget-conscious users who spend heavily on dining and groceries
First-time credit card holders looking for a no-frills starter card
Users with lower income who want to build a credit history with HDFC

faqs.question: What is the effective reward rate on dining and grocery?
faqs.answer: With 5X CashBack Points (5 points per ₹150) and each point worth ₹0.50, the effective return on dining and grocery is about 1.67%, which is decent for an entry-level card.

faqs.question: Is the annual fee easy to get waived?
faqs.answer: Yes, the ₹500 annual fee is waived on spending ₹50,000 in the previous year. With average monthly spending of ₹4,200, you can easily achieve the waiver.

faqs.question: Does the HDFC Freedom card have any welcome benefits?
faqs.answer: The HDFC Freedom card does not offer any significant welcome benefits. It is designed as a simple, everyday cashback card with low fees.

faqs.question: Can I upgrade from HDFC Freedom to a better card?
faqs.answer: Yes, with a good spending history and credit score, HDFC Bank may offer upgrades to cards like HDFC Millennia or MoneyBack Plus after 12-18 months of usage.
